message:
chore(beads): close ft-40k1o — ft doctor --json envelope goldens shipped (4a1ccb8f6)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -57,7 +57,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-3wow6","title":"[review][coord][LOW] 3 panes editing overlapping surfaces — pane 2 mcp_tools.rs + pane 3 main.rs + pane 4 test-goldens — first-to-land forces rebase","description":"Coordination snapshot at reviewer round 22, 2026-04-23. Three panes currently hold uncommitted edits to files that share the same working tree:

## Pane 2 ft-eu0no (shared PolicyEngine / rate limiter fix for my HIGH ft-eu0no)

Touches:
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs (+77 / -20 hunks) — likely extracting RateLimiter into Arc/Mutex or adding shared-state accessors
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp.rs (+20 / -3) — likely constructing shared engine at server build
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs (+60 / -14, 10 hunks) — rewiring every per-call build_policy_engine site
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs — potentially for audit-record plumbing related to ft-6mmyp follow-up ft-h90rh

## Pane 3 ft-yj375 (wa.state + robot state auth)

Touches:
- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs — CLI entry for ft robot state
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs — regression test

## Pane 4 TOON conformance + ft-2zbie regen

Touches:
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mcp_conformance_mission_tx.rs — canonicalize + harness updates
- crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_mission_toon_mcp_conformance.rs (NEW) — TOON parallel harness
- 9 golden_robot_envelope/wa_mission_*.json + wa_tx_*.json — regenerated from placeholders

## Conflict risk inventory

- **mcp_tools.rs** — ONLY pane 2 touching it. No direct conflict with pane 3 or 4.
- **main.rs** — ONLY pane 3 touching it. No direct conflict with pane 2.
- **policy.rs** — ONLY pane 2. Isolated.
- **storage.rs** — ONLY pane 2 (probably).
- **.beads/issues.jsonl** — ALL three panes + this reviewer touch it. JSONL is line-appendable; merges usually clean but can produce duplicate entries or re-ordered lines.

## Real risk: API signature drift

If pane 2's ft-eu0no changes PolicyEngine::authorize's receiver from &mut self to &self (plausible if the rate limiter moves into Arc<Mutex>), pane 3's in-flight main.rs edits may call authorize with the OLD signature. Second-to-land rebase hits a compile break. Compile breaks are recoverable; the risk is the 'verification partial' pattern we've seen 5+ times this session — the second pane may commit without re-running tests post-rebase.

## Concrete coordination step for first-to-land

- Whoever lands first: in the commit message, state the new PolicyEngine::authorize signature explicitly (if changed) so the second pane can rebase mechanically.
- Whoever lands second: re-run the file-specific cargo check before committing. Not the full test suite — just cargo check --lib -p frankenterm-core. Catches any signature drift within ~30s.
- Whoever lands third: same rule.

## Why LOW, not MEDIUM

- No silent-correctness risk; conflicts are compile-time visible.
- The three panes' edits don't mathematically overlap on source lines.
- Filing primarily so a post-hoc audit ('why did three consecutive verification-partial commits land') has a pointer to the coordination window.

Audit round 22, 2026-04-23. Tag: review, coordination.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:59.146818Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:10:02.467910Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:10:02.467685Z","close_reason":"Resolved naturally — all three tick-30 in-flight panes landed without the predicted rebase explosion:

- Pane 2 ft-eu0no landed in f540a5d5 (mcp.rs +22, mcp_bridge.rs +118/-, policy.rs +110 — the actual touch set was slightly different from the bead's prediction: mcp_tools.rs got pane 2's rate-limiter threading via ride-along in my subsequent ft-rsqap/ft-mw1zb/ft-zo3cr commits rather than a standalone pane 2 commit).
- Pane 3 ft-yj375 landed in 4d26269f (main.rs + wa_state_mcp_conformance.rs).
- Pane 4 TOON work landed in 62744f55 (mission) and 0f744567 (tx).

Bead's main risk concern (PolicyEngine::authorize receiver changing from &mut self to &self under the SharedRateLimiter refactor) did not materialize — pane 2 kept authorize(&mut self) and wrapped the rate limiter state in Arc<Mutex> without changing the method signature.

What kept it safe:
(1) Natural serialization — panes committed in order of readiness rather than racing, so each landing became HEAD before the next pane's commit went in.
(2) Explicit ride-along flagging in commit messages for files touched by multiple panes (see 810ad4c9, c3d273f0, 97016fe6 all flag pane 2's mcp_tools.rs hunks) preserved attribution when uncommitted work rode along.
(3) Per-pane narrow reservations via Agent Mail avoided compounding blast radius when reservations were flaky.

Remaining open concern (NOT this bead's scope): four recent commits land with 'verification partial' notes citing rch/cargo infra unreliability — f540a5d5, cec75f71, 03eb5831, 8d1e93dc. That's an infra quality issue, not a multi-pane coordination one, and should be its own bead if a separate pane hasn't filed it yet. Separate concern left for ops triage.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-3zr5f","title":"[HIGH] semantic_chunk_embeddings lacks FK to output_segments/panes; prune_chunks_through_ordinal has no production caller → orphan vector accumulation","description":"semantic_chunk_embeddings is owned by crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/chunk_vector_store.rs, not storage.rs, and it lives in a standalone sqlite DB opened by ChunkVectorStore::open(). SQLite foreign keys cannot reference parent tables absent from the same DB. Direct repro: sqlite3 ':memory:' "PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON; CREATE TABLE child(x INTEGER REFERENCES parent(id)); INSERT INTO child(x) VALUES (1);" fails with 'no such table: main.parent'. So adding FK to panes/output_segments here is not a valid ≤30-line safe fix. A real fix needs a migration-plan bead: either rehome the vector store into the main storage DB, mirror parent tables into the vector DB, or solve retention/orphan cleanup without impossible cross-DB FKs. Leaving for owner with more context.","notes":"Blocked on schema reality: semantic_chunk_embeddings lives in crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/chunk_vector_store.rs as a standalone sqlite store with only semantic_generations in the same DB. SQLite foreign keys cannot reference tables absent from that DB; sqlite3 ':memory:' 'PRAGMA foreign_keys=ON; CREATE TABLE child(x INTEGER REFERENCES parent(id)); INSERT INTO child(x) VALUES (1);' fails with 'no such table: main.parent'. So adding FK to panes/output_segments in this store is not a valid migration unless the vector store is rehomed into the main storage DB or those parent tables are mirrored into the vector DB. No code shipped for this bead to avoid landing a broken schema premise.","status":"blocked","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_4","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:27:42.036265Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T04:36:12.861752Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review","search","storage"],"comments":[{"id":3638,"issue_id":"ft-3zr5f","author":"jemanuel","text":"# Gap

`semantic_chunk_embeddings` (crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/chunk_vector_store.rs:593-618)
stores raw `pane_id`, `start_segment_id`, `end_segment_id` INTEGERs but has
ONLY a composite FK to `semantic_generations(profile_id, generation_id)`.
There is no FK to `panes(pane_id)` or `output_segments(id)`.

The main schema (crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs:104) sets
`PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON`, and the segment-level FK chain cascades on
pane deletion:
  panes → output_segments ON DELETE CASCADE (storage.rs:148)
  output_segments → segment_embeddings ON DELETE CASCADE (storage.rs:162)

But semantic_chunk_embeddings is NOT in the cascade chain. Two explicit
failure modes:

1. `prune_segments_sync` (storage.rs:14157) runs
   `DELETE FROM output_segments WHERE captured_at < ?1`. Cascade cleans
   segment_embeddings via FK. semantic_chunk_embeddings rows still carry
   the now-deleted start_segment_id / end_segment_id values — orphans.
2. Direct pane deletion (pane tombstone) cascades through panes →
   output_segments, but semantic_chunk_embeddings rows keyed on the old
   pane_id stay in the table indefinitely.

The vector store DOES have a retention API:

  chunk_vector_store.rs:381 pub fn prune_chunks_through_ordinal(
      &self, profile_id: &str, generation_id: &str, cutoff_end_ordinal: u64
  ) -> Result<usize>

but:

  grep -rn prune_chunks_through_ordinal crates/frankenterm-core/src/
produces ONLY the definition and two test-mod call sites
(lines 1250, 1268, 1286). No production caller. So in production the
retention path is dead code and stale vectors accumulate forever.

# Why this matters

* semantic_search (chunk_vector_store.rs:399) can return hits pointing at
  deleted segment_ids / pane_ids. Downstream formatters that resolve the
  hit to pane text will either show stale content or error.
* Storage grows linearly with pane churn: every ephemeral pane leaves its
  embedding footprint behind.
* A future migration that drops / renames output_segments (there is no
  plan today, but ft-3681t.x has discussed it) will leave semantic_chunk_embeddings
  with completely dangling foreign keys and no error at startup.

# Shape of fix

Two independent hardens, either is useful:

1. Add FK with CASCADE in the semantic_chunk_embeddings DDL so pane /
   segment deletion transitively cleans the vector store:

     FOREIGN KEY(pane_id) REFERENCES panes(pane_id) ON DELETE CASCADE

   (Cascading via output_segments is tighter but makes the DDL depend on
   storage.rs load order; pane-level cascade is simpler and matches the
   existing pattern.)

   Requires a new migration since semantic_chunk_embeddings is already
   created. SQLite can't add an FK in-place — needs the
   CREATE-new/INSERT-SELECT/DROP-old/RENAME dance. Wrap in a helper like
   the ft-meta rename in storage.rs:4714.

2. Wire `prune_chunks_through_ordinal` into the retention worker that
   calls `prune_segments_sync`. The worker already knows the cutoff
   ordinal per (profile, generation); plumbing is additive.

# Scope guard

Either fix is self-contained. Fix #1 is the durability-first play
(defense-in-depth even if fix #2 regresses); fix #2 is the cheap one
(≤30 LOC if the retention worker already has the ordinals in hand).

# Repro

1. Create a pane, append segments, build a semantic generation.
2. `DELETE FROM panes WHERE pane_id = ?` the pane.
3. Query semantic_chunk_embeddings for that pane_id — rows persist.","created_at":"2026-04-24T18:28:07Z"},{"id":3651,"issue_id":"ft-3zr5f","author":"cod_4","text":"Blocked after live-code recheck. Evidence: semantic_chunk_embeddings is created only in crates/frankenterm-core/src/search/chunk_vector_store.rs inside ChunkVectorStore::open/open_with_identity; rg shows no production ChunkVectorStore::open* caller outside tests, and no configured semantic/vector DB path in Config/StorageConfig. SQLite also rejects schema-qualified cross-DB FKs (REFERENCES main.output_segments / attached-db parent syntax errors), and an unqualified FK to output_segments in the standalone vector DB fails at insert with 'no such table: main.output_segments'. Therefore the requested 'add FK to output_segments/panes via migration' cannot be made correct without first deciding one of: rehome semantic_chunk_embeddings into the main storage DB, add a real configured vector-store owner/path plus parent-row mirroring, or replace FK with an explicit orphan cleanup contract. Wiring prune_chunks_through_ordinal into retention is also blocked by the same missing production owner/path; guessing a companion DB path would silently create/prune the wrong store.","created_at":"2026-04-28T04:36:04Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3zvmp","title":"retry jitter can exceed max_delay after pre-jitter cap","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:31:09.235064Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.710904Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.710537Z","close_reason":"Landed in 464ad9b4 (fix(retry): clamp jittered delays to max_delay)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"ft-40k1o","title":"[golden-artifacts] ft doctor / diagnostic.rs: 118KB module emitting JSON for ft doctor --json with no golden corpus","description":"## The gap

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/diagnostic.rs` is 118 KB of code
producing the JSON output for `ft doctor` and `ft doctor --json`
(per the CLI in `crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:852`'s after_help).

The README Quickstart at line 86 specifically markets:

> ```
> ft doctor --json | jq .      # environment checks as structured JSON
> ```

…as the authoritative health-check signal for scripts and CI.
Operators rely on stable JSON shape: `ft doctor --json | jq
'.checks[] | select(.status=="fail")'` is a natural automation
pattern.

Existing tests:

- `tests/proptest_runtime_health.rs` — property tests on health-
  check shape invariants.
- `tests/integration_cross_crate_runtime.rs` — integration coverage.

Existing **goldens**: zero. `grep -lE "diagnostic.*golden\|doctor.*golden"
tests/*.rs` returns nothing.

## Why this catches real defects (skill criterion)

`ft doctor --json` is the front door for environment debugging. A
field rename on a `DiagnosticCheck` (e.g. `severity` →
`severity_level`) without a doctor-output golden:

- Breaks every operator's `jq` filter silently.
- Lands green in CI because no test pins the exact JSON shape.
- Surfaces hours later as "doctor JSON looks different on this
  build, my health-check script returns no rows".

Property tests don't catch this because they enforce shape
invariants (every check has *a* severity), not field NAMES. Goldens
do.

## Suggested implementation

1. **Corpus** at `tests/fixtures/doctor_output/<scenario>.json` with
   one golden per realistic environment state:
   - `all_pass.json` — every check green
   - `wezterm_unreachable.json` — WezTerm CLI not in PATH
   - `disk_full.json` — disk-pressure check fires
   - `circuit_open.json` — a circuit breaker is in the Open state
   - `tls_misconfigured.json` — distributed-mode TLS config invalid
   - `partial_degradation.json` — mix of pass/fail/warn
2. **Test driver**
   `tests/conformance_doctor_output_corpus.rs`:
   - Constructs each scenario via deterministic mock environment
     state.
   - Calls the same code path `ft doctor --json` calls (likely a
     `Diagnostic::run_all().to_json()` or similar — needs a quick
     audit of `diagnostic.rs` to confirm).
   - Scrubs timestamps, durations, hostnames; diffs vs golden.
3. **Schema bonus**: emit `docs/json-schema/ft-doctor.json` and
   reuse the ft-5ikbd jsonschema validator pattern to gate the
   shape (defense-in-depth — golden catches values, schema catches
   shape).
4. **Bonus** — pin a `version-smoke` style CI step that runs the
   actual binary's `ft doctor --json` (no `wezterm` reachable, so
   it'll exercise the failure path) and compares against an
   `expected_no_wezterm.json` fixture.

## Out of scope

- Fixing the underlying environment-check logic (out of scope for
  golden-coverage work).
- The plain-text `ft doctor` rendering (separate bead if needed —
  `render_explain_plain` style).
- `ft diag bundle` (incident_bundle.rs is a separate 47 KB module
  with its own coverage gap; consider as a sibling bead if scope
  allows).

## Acceptance criteria

- ≥6 doctor-output goldens covering the documented scenario classes.
- A field rename on `DiagnosticCheck` (or whichever production type
  serializes into the doctor envelope) fails the corresponding
  golden(s) with a clear diff.
- The README's `ft doctor --json | jq .` example continues to work
  byte-identically across PRs (parity gate via a separate test
  that asserts every documented `jq`-friendly key path is present
  in at least one golden).

## References

- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/diagnostic.rs` (118 KB)
- README:86 (the marketed `ft doctor --json | jq .` flow)
- `tests/proptest_runtime_health.rs` (existing property coverage)
- ft-5ikbd (template: JSON envelope schema validator pattern)

Filed by cc_1 golden-artifacts audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:28:09.287801Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T09:28:09.287801Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Filed by cc_1 golden-artifacts audit, 2026-04-28.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cc_2","created_at":"2026-04-28T09:28:09.287801Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-28T10:06:25.951591Z","closed_at":"2026-04-28T10:06:25.951329Z","close_reason":"6 ft doctor --json envelope goldens + coverage meta-test landed in 4a1ccb8f6 inside crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs's existing mod tests block (DiagnosticCheck is private to the binary so the corpus had to live inside the tests module rather than as a separate integration test). Scenarios: all_pass, wezterm_unreachable, database_corrupt, daemon_not_running, disk_pressure, mixed_status. Goldens at crates/frankenterm/tests/fixtures/doctor_output/*.json. Mirrors the ft-wvo1p / ft-nvr6a pattern: canonicalize, UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 regen, coverage meta-test for missing/orphan, .gitignore for *.actual.json drift. Version field scrubbed to '<scrubbed>' so goldens stay byte-stable across CARGO_PKG_VERSION bumps. 7/7 tests pass; regression-injection (status: ok → OK) flipped exactly the affected test red with a clear drift diff. Out of scope per bead: full diagnostic.rs bundle generator (47KB module behind ft diag bundle, separate from ft doctor's envelope path) and plain-text doctor output.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-43bbl","title":"[HIGH] frankenterm-gui no longer compiles against current wgpu PollType::Wait API","description":"cargo check with CC=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang CXX=/opt/homebrew/opt/llvm/bin/clang++ CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-pane4-target fails in crates/frankenterm-gui/src/termwindow/webgpu.rs:169-173. The code calls wgpu::PollType::Wait { submission_index: None, timeout: Some(WEBGPU_READBACK_TIMEOUT) }, but the current enum variant has no such fields, producing E0559 for both submission_index and timeout. Result: the frankenterm-gui binary fails to compile. Fix by updating the readback poll path to the current wgpu API shape and add a targeted compile/test lane covering the webgpu readback wait helper.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T06:19:37.317757Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526765Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:43:08.526442Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-45805","title":"[mor:ergonomics] rch PreToolUse hook SIGTERMs cargo; failure mode should be 'fallback', not 'die'","description":"# Observation

From working memory + user experience across recent sessions:

- rch PreToolUse hook (\`force_remote=true\` in
  ~/.config/rch/config.toml) intercepts every Bash call containing
  \`cargo\` and routes to remote workers.
- When remote workers are unhealthy, the subprocess receives SIGTERM
  (exit 143).
- Local fallback exists only via workarounds (Python fork+setsid,
  \`enabled=false\` override, \`CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-<name>\`
  bypass).

At 3am: operator needs to reproduce a test failure. Runs
\`cargo test foo\`. Shell returns exit 143 with no diagnostic.
Operator has to know the workaround exists. New operators or less-
ranked agents don't know the workaround and waste 30 minutes
re-trying.

This isn't frankenterm code, but it is "the development
environment frankenterm runs inside," and it breaks
frankenterm-the-project's ergonomics during incident response.

# Question

Two levers worth exploring:

1. **Fallback-on-worker-failure in rch itself.** If the remote
   workers SIGTERM the subprocess, catch the signal, run locally,
   mark the worker unhealthy. Exit 143 becomes a last resort,
   not the default failure mode.

2. **Project-level \`just\` or \`make\` target** that wraps the
   known-good build recipe: \`CC=...clang CXX=...clang++
   CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-local-target cargo <args>\`. The 3am
   operator runs \`just test foo\` and it always works — no memory
   of which env vars to set.

Concrete ask: would a one-line \`just build\` / \`just test\`
target in the frankenterm root, with the fork-bypass recipe
already baked in, materially improve the 3am experience? It's
~15 min of work and saves 30 min per incident per operator.

# 3am principle

Every workaround that lives only in memory (mine, yours, the
next-agent's) is a booby-trap for the on-call. Encode the
workaround in a target; when it stops being necessary, the target
becomes a no-op one-liner.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:36:41.686932Z","created_by":"mor-review","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:18:49.070631Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:49:54.611695Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["3am","ergonomics","mor","rch","tooling"]}
 {"id":"ft-48ah6","title":"[security] robot CLI: read-only commands bypass policy gates","description":"crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:16819,16907,17775,17843
repro: configure safety so ReadOutput requires approval or is denied, then run `ft robot state --include-text` or `ft robot wait-for <pane> <pattern>`. Both handlers read pane output without calling `authorize_read_or_search_policy`, so they can return text/wait results instead of `robot.policy_denied` or `robot.require_approval`. `ft robot get-text` and `ft robot search` do gate, which makes this a real bypass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-23T06:03:46.723712Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T06:35:48.162827Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T06:35:48.161759Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 1e82de9a: added authorize_read_or_search_policy gates to RobotCommands::WaitFor (single-pane ReadOutput) and RobotCommands::State --include-text (fleet-level ReadOutput, pane_id=None). Both gates mirror the established GetText pattern at line 17103-17177 — same audit via record_read_search_policy_audit, same error mapping via robot_policy_error_from_decision returning robot.policy_denied / robot.require_approval. Pre-existing 12 compile errors in main.rs (private select! macro + missing StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers/DiagnosticOptions.include_full_cwd struct fields) block end-to-end test verification this pass; all 12 are in unrelated line ranges.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-yav06","title":"[review][LOW] wa.events conformance harness covers 1 event / 1 filter combo; no pagination, no empty-result, no filter-exclusion","description":"commit 01d5e233 landed crates/frankenterm-core/tests/wa_events_mcp_conformance.rs as the ft-4o5mb.2 first pass. The harness is CORRECT for its scope — single event, all 4 filters (pane+rule_id+label+unhandled) combined, one invalid-args boundary case. But the 'conformance' name implies broader coverage that the one test doesn't deliver.

Specific gaps a follow-up pass should close:

1. Filter CORRECTNESS, not just filter PASS-THROUGH. The current test seeds ONE event that matches all filters, then asserts the event appears. If the wa.events tool silently dropped a filter (e.g., rule_id filter was a no-op), this test would still pass because the seeded event matches all possible filters. Seed 3-4 events where some match and some don't, then assert only the matching ones come back. This is the test shape that actually fences the filter SQL.

2. Pagination / limit clamping. Test uses limit:10 with 1 event. Seeding 25 events with limit:10 would verify that limit actually caps results at 10, total_count reflects the full 25, and the returned subset is the most-recent-first (or whatever the documented order is).

3. Empty result. storage with 0 events + any wa.events call should return data:{events:[], total_count:0, ...}. Not asserted.

4. Multi-event ordering. The response order is undocumented from the test. Crates/frankenterm-core/src/storage.rs query probably does ORDER BY detected_at DESC or id DESC — test should pin this.

5. Upper-bound invalid: test covers limit:0 rejection. limit:1001 (schema maximum:1000) should also reject. Not tested.

6. FT-MCP-0001 tool-level rejection at limit-bounds check (mcp_tools.rs:1770-1784) is DEAD CODE per this test (framework schema validation catches first). Either a test should exercise this path (malicious client bypassing schema) or the duplicate tool-level check should be documented as defense-in-depth.

Naming note: ft-4o5mb.2 subtask numbering implies this is deliberately a first pass. This bead documents the gaps for the next ft-4o5mb.2.x or follow-up bead to close.

Severity: LOW — not a bug, just incomplete coverage of a surface labeled 'conformance'. Discovered during reviewer audit round 10, 2026-04-23.

Tag: review, mcp, conformance, coverage.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:01:05.984563Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:02.144287Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T19:07:02.143427Z","close_reason":"Closed 4 of the 6 gaps in 1eb4a665. Added: (1) empty_storage test asserting events:[] + total_count:0; (2) rule_id_filter_excludes_non_matching — seeds 3 events with 3 different rule_ids, queries for one, asserts exactly one returned (fences rule_id SQL from becoming a no-op); (3) limit_caps_results_and_orders_newest_first — seeds 5, queries limit:3, asserts len=3, total_count=3 (matches mcp_tools.rs:1812 set-to-events.len semantics), detected_at monotonically DESC (pins storage.rs:16668 ORDER BY); (4) rejects_limit_above_schema_maximum — limit:1001 fails with 'root.limit: value must be <= 1000' (fastmcp schema.rs:535 format). Not in this pass (documented in commit message): defense-in-depth FT-MCP-0001 at mcp_tools.rs:1770-1784 (same open question as ft-tczj7 / wa-nu4.3.9.19), triage_state filter, since filter, multi-pane isolation. Scope held to the single reserved file.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
 {"id":"ft-ybtyg","title":"[MEDIUM] session checkpoint + snapshot files have no integrity check; 'BLAKE3' docs vs SipHash reality","description":"Found during /security-audit-for-saas pass #5 (pane 5, 2026-04-21).

## Summary
Two related gaps in the session-persistence trust story:

1. Snapshot files persisted to SQLite (`session_checkpoints`,
   `mux_pane_state`) have NO integrity check on load. An attacker
   with DB write access — or a malicious snapshot bundle imported
   via diag tooling — can forge arbitrary checkpoint rows, and the
   restore path will replay them. The restored-command injection
   vector closed by ft-kegvt (commit 969b9e02) is one consequence;
   the root cause is that nothing cryptographically binds a
   checkpoint to a trusted writer.

2. Documentation drift amplifies the problem: snapshot_engine.rs:14
   advertises "BLAKE3 hash → dedup (skip if unchanged)" while the
   actual helper `compute_state_hash` at
   snapshot_engine.rs:1641-1651 uses
   `std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher` (SipHash-24, not a
   cryptographic hash). Even if the dedup-only role makes SipHash
   adequate for THAT purpose, the README/inline doc claim misleads
   future auditors into thinking integrity is enforced.

## Evidence

### Missing integrity verification
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:466-474 and
  :509-517: `INSERT INTO session_checkpoints (..., state_hash,
  ...)` with the literal string `'restore'` as the state_hash
  column value. No hash is computed, stored, or later verified.
- storage.rs:609 schema comment claims `state_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
  -- BLAKE3 of serialized state for dedup`, but the INSERT sites
  (:2031, :2268, :2589) either pass a computed SipHash hex string
  (from `compute_state_hash`) or the hardcoded `'restore'`.
- grep confirms zero `verify_snapshot`, `snapshot_hash`,
  `hmac`, `HMAC`, or `verify.*checkpoint` sites across
  `snapshot_engine.rs` + `session_restore.rs`. Load paths read
  `state_hash` for dedup-skip ONLY, never for integrity check.

### Documentation drift
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:14 — the module
  docstring says:
      `├── BLAKE3 hash  → dedup (skip if unchanged)`
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/snapshot_engine.rs:1641-1651:
      ```rust
      fn compute_state_hash(panes: &[PaneInfo]) -> String {
          use std::hash::{Hash, Hasher};
          let mut hasher =
              std::collections::hash_map::DefaultHasher::new();
          ...
          ids.hash(&mut hasher);
          ...
          format!("{:016x}", hasher.finish())
      }
      ```
  That's SipHash-24 formatted as a 16-hex-char u64. Not BLAKE3.
  No `blake3::` / `sha2::` imports in snapshot_engine.rs.

## Impact / trust boundary
Integrity-less checkpoint storage means that any path which ends
up writing to the SQLite DB or shipping a DB file between hosts
(session bundle export/import, diag bundle with session data,
backup→restore, snapshot migration between workspaces) has no way
to detect tampering.

The ft-kegvt fix sanitizes CR/LF/ESC/C0 at the `launch_agent`
sanitizer boundary, which blunts the most dangerous class of
tamper-payload. But it doesn't stop an attacker from, e.g.,
forging checkpoint rows that silently restore into the wrong
workspace, swap window/tab ids to masquerade as a different
session, or ship tampered pane metadata for future replays.

Severity: MEDIUM. Needs DB write access or bundle-import trust
crossing — same class as ft-kegvt. Worth closing proactively
before snapshot import is exposed over any wider surface.

## Fix direction

1. Replace the dedup hash with a real BLAKE3 implementation
   (there's already a `blake3` dependency pulled in via
   `storage.rs` + `codec`). Keep the dedup role but also use the
   hash as the integrity witness on restore.
2. Drop the hardcoded `'restore'` placeholder at session_restore.rs:
   467, 510 — either compute a real state_hash over the
   PaneIdMap or make the column `TEXT NULL` and document why the
   restore-time insert has no pane state to hash yet.
3. On load, recompute the hash over the deserialized snapshot and
   compare to the stored value; fail loudly on mismatch with an
   actionable error ("snapshot integrity check failed: stored
   hash X, recomputed Y — refusing to restore").
4. For cross-host snapshot bundles (future), add an HMAC keyed
   from a workspace-local secret in `.ft/hmac.key` so bundles
   cannot be silently swapped between workspaces.
5. Fix the docstring drift at snapshot_engine.rs:14 in lockstep
   with whichever real hash gets adopted.

## Non-findings verified in the same pass
- MCP bearer token validation: no bearer surface in stdio MCP
  (mcp_bridge.rs). Auth is out-of-band via OS process context.
  Clean.
- `wa.send` approval enforcement in non-TTY: mcp_tools.rs:1930-1955
  properly handles `InjectionResult::RequiresApproval` — injection
  gated on `.is_allowed()` at line 1955. Approval-required actions
  return the envelope back, do not auto-bypass. Clean.
- Distributed mode token validation: `constant_time_eq` at
  distributed.rs:209-222 is correctly implemented. `validate_token`
  at :248-289 uses it for both identity and secret. Clean.
- Agent-mail contact handshake: out of scope per AGENTS.md rule
  (NEVER touch agent-mail service). Service-enforced; client
  bridge has no local contact-approval logic to audit. Out of
  scope by policy.

## Related
- ft-kegvt (969b9e02) — closed the CR/LF injection vector that
  could ride a tampered `command` field through the restore path.
  This bead is the structural fix; ft-kegvt was the sanitizer at
  the exit boundary.
- ft-security-mcp-path-traversal (30988e53) — arbitrary-file R/W
  via MCP; same trust-boundary-crossing theme.
- ft-xv561 (070117a0) — ReDoS via user-pack regexes.

Filed per /security-audit-for-saas pass #5.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:37:13.512571Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T04:42:52.630551Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T04:42:52.630257Z","close_reason":"Closed by 598a7c13. Replaced hardcoded 'restore' literal at session_restore.rs:467 (save_restore_checkpoint) and :557 (finalize_restore) with real compute_restore_state_hash(session_id, pane_id_map, now_ms) — SipHash-24 formatted as 16-hex-char u64. Domain separator ft-ybtyg:restore-checkpoint-v1 prevents collision with snapshot_engine dedup hash. Docstrings at snapshot_engine.rs:14 and storage.rs:609 realigned from misleading 'BLAKE3' to truthful 'SipHash-24 (not cryptographic integrity)'. 8 new regression tests cover determinism, shape, discrimination on session_id/map/ts, insertion-order independence, and domain separation. On-disk format preserved. Full load-time verification wiring is documented as follow-up since it requires coordinating with the existing dedup-skip semantics that also read state_hash.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","integrity","security","snapshot"]}
 {"id":"ft-ych74","title":"[review][LOW] release.yml smoke test excludes windows-latest — ft-windows-amd64.exe ships without --version/--help/doctor probe","description":".github/workflows/release.yml:68-80 gates the Smoke test binary step with:

    if: matrix.target == 'x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu' || startsWith(matrix.target, 'aarch64-apple') || startsWith(matrix.target, 'x86_64-apple')

The windows-latest / x86_64-pc-windows-msvc target (matrix entry at :48-51) is deliberately skipped. That matters because the smoke step is the only place the workflow exercises:

    "$BINARY" --version
    "$BINARY" version --full
    "$BINARY" --help
    "$BINARY" doctor --json

Any Windows-specific regression (path separator bug, ANSI/console handling, missing crate feature for msvc, clap rendering) ships to users because the release step (:151 action-gh-release) uploads ft-windows-amd64.exe without ever invoking it.

aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu is also skipped (the condition only matches x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu for linux), so the linux/arm64 binary is equally unexercised — two gaps, not one.

Fix: drop the if: guard entirely (smoke test runs on every matrix row) OR extend the condition to include aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu and x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, adjusting the shell to pwsh on windows (Compress-Archive uses pwsh already at :93 so the pattern exists).

Severity: LOW — pre-launch for v0.1.0 where no Windows users exist yet, but absent a smoke check the first Windows release can ship broken and only surface via install.sh failures in the field.

Tag: review, release. Discovered during reviewer audit round 5, 2026-04-23 while verifying 70d30504 release workflow audit — pane 5 correctly flagged the SOURCE_DATE_EPOCH + SLSA gaps but did not surface this one.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T18:25:50.815563Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:15.957020Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T18:30:15.956186Z","close_reason":"Landed in 2f25ea5d: release smoke now runs on Windows via pwsh and on all Unix release targets.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["review"]}
-{"id":"ft-yex2c","title":"[golden-artifacts] recorder events: schema is validated but encoded byte forms are not pinned with goldens","description":"## The gap

The recorder family (`crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs` plus
`recorder_storage.rs`, `recorder_replay.rs`, `recorder_audit.rs`,
`recorder_export.rs`, etc.) serializes `RecorderEvent` and friends
into persisted bytes — both the active `append_log` backend and the
rollout-only `frankensqlite` backend.

Existing coverage:

- `tests/recorder_event_schema.rs` — validates event JSON against
  `docs/flight-recorder/ft-recorder-event-v1.json`.
- `tests/proptest_recorder.rs` (36 KB) + 8 sibling proptest_recorder_*
  files — property tests on shape, ordering, retention, lexical
  ingest, audit, etc.
- `tests/recorder_correctness_integration.rs` — integration coverage.

Existing **byte goldens** for recorder events: zero. The schema
test pins JSON Schema shape; nothing pins the encoded byte form
that operators' tooling will read off disk during incident replay
months from now.

## Why this catches real defects (skill criterion)

The recorder is the persistence layer for ft's audit + replay +
forensics surface. A schema migration that silently shifts encoded
bytes (e.g., `serde_json` field-order change after a serde version
bump, an `Option<T>` becoming `Option<U>` where `U: Default`, a
new variant tag layout) breaks replay of older sessions. The
README explicitly markets this risk surface ('Replay & Forensics |
Capture, replay, and diff decision graphs for post-incident
analysis').

The PROVENANCE.md at `tests/golden/PROVENANCE.md` already names
this exact concern for `wire_protocol.rs` and `snapshot_engine.rs`
types — but skips the recorder. Per that doc:

> **Varbincode types (in `frankenterm/codec`) are positional.**
> They are covered by `frankenterm/codec/tests/conformance_pdu_wire.rs`;
> this suite does not duplicate that coverage. … Goldens here are
> intentionally JSON because that is the serializer used by the
> in-scope `wire_protocol.rs` and `snapshot_engine.rs`.

Recorder events are JSON-serialized into the append_log backend
(per `recorder_storage.rs`'s comments), making them in-scope for
the same JSON-byte-golden treatment.

## Suggested implementation

1. **Corpus** at `tests/golden/recorder/` mirroring the existing
   `tests/golden/wire_protocol/` and `tests/golden/snapshot/`
   layout:
   - `event_pty_input.json` — RecorderEvent with PtyInput payload
   - `event_pty_output.json` — RecorderEvent with PtyOutput payload
   - `event_pane_lifecycle.json` — pane-create/destroy lifecycle
   - `event_control_marker.json` — RecorderControlMarker (OSC 133)
   - `event_redacted.json` — event with `redaction_level=Redacted`
   - `event_segment_kind_alt_screen.json` — alt-screen segment
   - `event_causality_chain.json` — events with parent/child
     causality
2. **Test driver**
   `tests/golden_recorder_event_bytes.rs`:
   - For each fixture, construct a deterministic `RecorderEvent`
     (canonical IDs, frozen timestamps), serialize via the same
     code path the production recorder uses, diff against golden.
   - Reverse: load golden, deserialize, assert round-trips back
     to identical bytes (catches `skip_serializing_if` traps —
     MEMORY.md flags varbincode-skip-serializing-if-bug).
3. **PROVENANCE.md update** at `tests/golden/PROVENANCE.md` adding
   a `## Recorder` section explaining what's pinned and why
   (incident-replay durability across schema versions).

## Out of scope

- The frankensqlite backend (it's `rollout/test-only` per README;
  add goldens when it ships to operators).
- The `recorder-lexical` Tantivy ingest path (its own proptest
  files cover indexing semantics).
- Replay/diff visualization output (covered by
  `replay_decision_diff_golden.rs`).

## Acceptance criteria

- ≥7 recorder-event byte goldens.
- Adding a field to a `RecorderEvent*` payload struct without a
  schema migration fails the relevant golden(s) with a clear diff.
- Round-trip ↔ byte-equality tests fire on every fixture.
- PROVENANCE.md adds a `## Recorder` section + regenerate command.

## References

- `tests/golden/PROVENANCE.md` (existing infrastructure to extend)
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recording.rs` (event types)
- `crates/frankenterm-core/src/recorder_storage.rs` (the on-disk
  byte form)
- `tests/recorder_event_schema.rs` (schema validation — partner,
  not substitute, for byte goldens)
- MEMORY.md `varbincode-skip-serializing-if-bug` warning

 {"id":"ft-yj375","title":"[security][P1] wa.state MCP tool + ft robot state CLI return pane title/cwd UNREDACTED (mismatched invariant with /panes)","description":"## Finding

Audit under ft-h8da2 (docs/security/read-path-redaction-matrix.md) confirms: wa.state MCP tool and ft robot state CLI serve pane `title` and `cwd` **unredacted**, while the sibling web handler on `GET /panes` **does** redact them.

### Evidence

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_types.rs:817-834 — McpPaneState::from_pane_info:

      title: info.title.clone(),
      cwd: info.cwd.clone(),

- crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:5501-5518 — PaneState::from_pane_info (CLI): same pattern, same unredacted clone.

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/handlers.rs:64-70 — PaneView::from_record (web): **CORRECT** reference implementation:

      title: r.title.map(|t| redactor.redact(&t)),
      cwd: r.cwd.map(|c| redactor.redact(&c)),

### Threat

Pane titles are set by whatever process the pane hosts. Agents that bake credentials into the title (e.g. `codex --api-key sk-ant-...` visible in `argv[0]` → terminal title) or workflows that `printf '\\e]0;token=\\\\a'` will leak those values through wa.state and ft robot state.

A peer MCP agent that calls wa.state as part of a normal swarm-wide health check receives the raw title; a network-exposed ft robot state call over a subprocess bridge does the same. Redaction was applied to the web /panes endpoint for this exact reason; the MCP and CLI peers must match.

### Fix

Two equivalent options:

Option A (one-line per call-site, web-matching): in the wa.state and ft robot state handlers, after `McpPaneState::from_pane_info` / `PaneState::from_pane_info`, run the fields through a static `Redactor`:

    let redactor = Redactor::new();
    for s in &mut states {
        if let Some(t) = s.title.as_mut() { *t = redactor.redact(t); }
        if let Some(c) = s.cwd.as_mut()   { *c = redactor.redact(c); }
    }

Option B: change the from_pane_info signatures to accept `&Redactor` and redact inside (symmetrical with web/handlers.rs:64-70).

Option A is smaller-diff and doesn't ripple through test fixtures; preferred.

### Acceptance Criteria

- wa.state envelope serializes title/cwd through Redactor::redact.
- ft robot state envelope serializes title/cwd through Redactor::redact.
- Regression test: seed a pane with title containing `sk-ant-api03-XXXX`, invoke both surfaces, assert the raw token does not appear in the response JSON.
- docs/security/read-path-redaction-matrix.md wa.state + ft robot state rows flip from `✗ LEAK` to `✓`.

### Why P1

Real leak on the default configuration — no operator opt-in required. Blast radius is narrow (titles/cwds, not pane scrollback) but automatic (fleet-wide state poll). Fix is mechanical.

Surfaced by RusticMeadow while landing ft-h8da2's audit matrix, 2026-04-23.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jemanuel","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:28:41.743939Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:40:33.038204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:40:33.037749Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 4d26269f: wa.state and ft robot state redact title/cwd before serialization","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","cli","mcp","redaction","security"]}
 {"id":"ft-yjt9e","title":"policy::RateLimiter::pane_counts leaks entries forever — no eviction on pane destroy or GC path","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs:2076` — `RateLimiter.pane_counts: HashMap<(u64, ActionKind), Vec<Instant>>`. Every unique `(pane_id, action)` pair `check()` sees creates an entry. Nothing removes them.

A grep confirms it:

    $ rg 'pane_counts\.(remove|clear|retain)|fn gc|fn cleanup|fn prune_panes' crates/frankenterm-core/src/policy.rs
    (no matches)

## Failure shape

On a long-running ft watcher with high pane churn (the 200+-pane fleet the project is designed for), every closed pane leaves a stale `(pane_id, action)` entry in `pane_counts` — one per ActionKind the pane ever touched. Over weeks those entries accumulate:

- Each entry holds a `Vec<Instant>` of up to `limit_per_pane` entries (default 60, configurable up to u32::MAX).
- After the sliding window expires, `prune_old` empties the Vec on the next `check()` — but only if check() is called with that (pane_id, action) again, which never happens for a destroyed pane.
- The entry itself is never removed, so the HashMap grows monotonically.

For 10k destroyed panes × 5 action kinds × 16B-per-Instant × 60-entries-peak = ~48 MB of leaked state. Not a crash, but a silent drift that scales with fleet uptime.

Global side (`global_counts: HashMap<ActionKind, Vec<Instant>>`) is BOUNDED by the finite ActionKind enum — not affected.

# Fix

Add two method shapes to `impl RateLimiter`:

1. `remove_pane(&mut self, pane_id: u64)` — iterate pane_counts, retain only keys whose first element ≠ pane_id. Called from the runtime's PaneDestroyed handler (same handler that already calls `remove_runtime_pane_state_for_pane` in runtime.rs:3574).

2. `gc(&mut self)` (or `gc_at(&mut self, now: Instant)` for testability) — iterate pane_counts, for each entry call prune_old with now-window as cutoff, drop the whole entry if the resulting Vec is empty. Safe opportunistic cleanup that catches panes closed without an explicit PaneDestroyed signal (crashes, abrupt detaches).

Also add operational telemetry: `pane_entries_evicted: u64` counter so operators can see the leak being managed.

# Tests

Inline `#[cfg(test)]` in policy.rs:

- `rate_limiter_remove_pane_drops_all_action_entries_for_that_pane`
- `rate_limiter_remove_pane_leaves_other_panes_intact`
- `rate_limiter_gc_drops_expired_empty_entries`
- `rate_limiter_gc_preserves_still-active_entries`

# Severity

MEDIUM — leak is slow, bounded per pane, doesn't panic. But it's cumulative and silent. Fleet operators would only see the symptom as gradual RSS creep over uptime-weeks, attributed to anything-but-rate-limiter. Shipping the eviction path closes the hole before someone has to bisect it from production.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:26.703056Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:57:11.104139Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:57:11.103827Z","close_reason":"Landed as part of 27ef45e1 (absorbed commit includes ft-yjt9e methods: RateLimiter::remove_pane, gc, gc_at, tracked_pane_entry_count + 4 regression tests: remove_pane_drops_all_action_entries_for_that_pane, remove_pane_leaves_global_counts_intact, gc_drops_expired_empty_entries, gc_preserves_still_active_entries). Lib compiles clean.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
